title: USA 250th - Semiquincentennial
description: 250 years of American independence, celebrated alongside the 2026 FIFA World Cup on home soil.

facts.fact: First state to ratify the Constitution — December 7, 1787. Hosting World Cup watch parties in Wilmington.
Birthplace of the Declaration of Independence and the Constitution. Philadelphia's Lincoln Financial Field hosts knockout matches.
Third to ratify. MetLife Stadium — the FINAL venue — sits in East Rutherford, NJ. The biggest game on the biggest birthday.
Peach State hosts World Cup quarterfinals at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta.
The Constitution State. Gillette Stadium in nearby Foxborough, MA hosts quarterfinal action.
Home of the American Revolution. Gillette Stadium brings World Cup fever to New England.
Star-Spangled Banner state. Close to Philadelphia's knockout matches at Lincoln Financial Field.
Palmetto State. Fans travel up to Atlanta for quarterfinal action at Mercedes-Benz Stadium.
Live Free or Die. Ninth to ratify, hosting watch parties across the Granite State.
Old Dominion — home of presidents. A short drive to DC watch parties and Philadelphia matches.
Empire State. The FINAL at MetLife is practically a home game for NYC — the biggest event in American soccer history.
Tar Heel State. First in flight, first in World Cup fan zones from Charlotte to Raleigh.
Little Rhody — last of the 13 to ratify, first in World Cup passion per square mile.

trump_facts: President Donald J. Trump will personally welcome all 48 teams to the greatest World Cup in history.
The 250th birthday of the USA and the World Cup — two HUGE wins, folks. Nobody does events like us.
MetLife Stadium — right across the river from Trump Tower — will host the BIGGEST final ever.
America First on the pitch. The USMNT is ready to make soccer GREAT again.
250 years of winning. Add a World Cup trophy. That's what I call a tremendous celebration.
